issue.title: Retire remaining WP_Http::request branch public shells

artifacts.role: WPHX PHP WordPress-profile request adapter with named helper roles for retired branch shells
compiler input declaring the shared original-path WP_Http::request public shell helper metadata
shared runner support for compiling, installing, and validating the WPHX request shell
refreshed audit showing zero remaining request branch shell gaps
program matrix updated with WPHX-312.99 shell retirement status and bounded non-claims

claims: Thirteen bounded WP_Http::request branch candidates now install the shared WPHX PHP compiler-emitted original-path class-wp-http.php shell.

The shared request shell records named helper roles for header parsing, proxy options/authentication, error response, cookie options, stream destination/default-filename handling, unsafe URL validation, bad-protocol stripping, redirection-copy, null-header normalization, http_response attachment, and mbstring reset.
The public-shell gap audit now requires zero remaining WP_Http::request branch shell gaps.
non_claims: This retirement does not claim full WP_Http::request ownership.
This retirement does not claim whole-file WP_Http ownership, live HTTP transport behavior, installed distribution behavior, or upstream HTTP PHPUnit parity.
This retirement does not promote copied feed, embed, cron, or mail oracle public surfaces to durable generated public PHP ownership.
